2026-04-22T20:03:27.4058881Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T20:03:27.4059293Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T20:03:27.4059659Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T20:03:27.4059978Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

2026-04-22T20:07:23.2451374Z [2026-04-22T20:03:30Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 11883b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"tools":[{"description":"Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). 2026-04-22T20:07:23.2960949Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T20:04:24.193658814Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58517,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and x-request-id correlation in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58396 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58396 added the following to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (released as `1.0.0-beta.23`):\\n- **New public type `PlatformHeaders`** — a static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`)\\n- **`RequestIdMiddleware`** — sets the `x-request-id` response header automatically on every HTTP response (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID)\\n- **`RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** — propagates `x-request-id` into `Activity.Baggage` for distributed tracing correlation\\n- **Simplified Tier 3 API** — replaced 6 individual `Add/Use` extension methods with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair\\n- **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName`; use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a **new public API** but is absent from the README Key Concepts section — Tier 3 users and protocol-package authors who reference header name constants will not discover it from the README\\n- The `x-request-id` automatic correlation behavior (RequestIdMiddleware sets it on every response using OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback) is undocumented beyond a one-line changelog entry; users debugging distributed traces need this explained\\n- The README Key Concepts section still lists `AddAgentServerVersion()` / `AddAgentServerLogging()` in the Logging paragraph but does not explain the new unified `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair, which replaces six calls for Tier 3 setups\\n- No snippet or example shows how to read or use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or Tier 3 setups\\n- The `samples/Sample2_Configuration.md` sample file does not mention `PlatformHeaders` or the `x-request-id` correlation pattern\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd a new **`PlatformHeaders`** entry to the Key Concepts section (after the `Health endpoint` subsection and before the `## Examples` section):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\n`PlatformHeaders` is a static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer platform. Protocol packages and custom middleware should reference these constants rather than hard-coding header name strings.\\n\\n| Constant | Header | Direction |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request (client correlation) / Response (always set) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response (server identity) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response (resolved session) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request (W3C Trace Context) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request (Azure SDK correlation) |\\n\\nThe `x-request-id` header is automatically set on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (included in both `AgentHostBuilder` and the `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` Tier 3 setup). The value is resolved as: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → new GUID. Downstream code can read the resolved value from `HttpContext.Items[PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey]`.\\n```\\n\\nAlso, update the existing **Logging** paragraph in Key Concepts to clarify `AddAgentServerCore()`:\\n\\nFind and replace this sentence:\\n```\\nfor Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it.\\n```\\nwith:\\n```\\nfor Tier 3 (standalone ASP.NET Core hosts), call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()`. These two calls replace the six individual extension methods that existed in earlier beta versions and register request-ID correlation, version headers, and inbound request logging in one step.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new snippet demonstrating `PlatformHeaders` usage in a Tier 3 (standalone) setup.\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample3_PlatformHeaders.cs`\\n\\nCreate this file:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core;\\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder;\\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample3_PlatformHeaders\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Illustrative sample — not a runnable test\\u0026#34;)]\\n    public void PlatformHeaders_Tier3Setup()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders\\n        var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\\n\\n        // AddAgentServerCore registers RequestIdMiddleware, server-version header, and inbound logging.\\n        builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\\n\\n        var app = builder.Build();\\n        app.UseAgentServerCore();\\n\\n        // Read the resolved request-correlation ID from HttpContext.Items in your own middleware.\\n        app.Use(async (context, next) =\\u0026gt;\\n        {\\n            if (context.Items.TryGetValue(PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey, out var requestId))\\n            {\\n                // requestId matches the x-request-id response header for this request.\\n                context.Response.Headers[PlatformHeaders.RequestId] = requestId?.ToString();\\n            }\\n            await next(context);\\n        });\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd a snippet placeholder in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` inside the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Using PlatformHeaders constants in a Tier 3 setup\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. The changes above are additions within existing sections, so no sections need to be created or reordered.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content from the new test file into the README placeholder\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm the API listing is current\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T20:07:23.2991281Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T20:04:24.20706293Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58517,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-22T20:07:23.3027506Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T20:04:24.365063385Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58517,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and x-request-id correlation in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58396 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58396 added the following to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (released as `1.0.0-beta.23`):\\n- **New public type `PlatformHeaders`** — a static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`)\\n- **`RequestIdMiddleware`** — sets the `x-request-id` response header automatically on every HTTP response (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID)\\n- **`RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** — propagates `x-request-id` into `Activity.Baggage` for distributed tracing correlation\\n- **Simplified Tier 3 API** — replaced 6 individual `Add/Use` extension methods with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair\\n- **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName`; use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a **new public API** but is absent from the README Key Concepts section — Tier 3 users and protocol-package authors who reference header name constants will not discover it from the README\\n- The `x-request-id` automatic correlation behavior (RequestIdMiddleware sets it on every response using OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback) is undocumented beyond a one-line changelog entry; users debugging distributed traces need this explained\\n- The README Key Concepts section still lists `AddAgentServerVersion()` / `AddAgentServerLogging()` in the Logging paragraph but does not explain the new unified `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair, which replaces six calls for Tier 3 setups\\n- No snippet or example shows how to read or use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or Tier 3 setups\\n- The `samples/Sample2_Configuration.md` sample file does not mention `PlatformHeaders` or the `x-request-id` correlation pattern\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd a new **`PlatformHeaders`** entry to the Key Concepts section (after the `Health endpoint` subsection and before the `## Examples` section):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\n`PlatformHeaders` is a static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer platform. Protocol packages and custom middleware should reference these constants rather than hard-coding header name strings.\\n\\n| Constant | Header | Direction |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request (client correlation) / Response (always set) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response (server identity) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response (resolved session) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request (W3C Trace Context) |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request (Azure SDK correlation) |\\n\\nThe `x-request-id` header is automatically set on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (included in both `AgentHostBuilder` and the `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` Tier 3 setup). The value is resolved as: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → new GUID. Downstream code can read the resolved value from `HttpContext.Items[PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey]`.\\n```\\n\\nAlso, update the existing **Logging** paragraph in Key Concepts to clarify `AddAgentServerCore()`:\\n\\nFind and replace this sentence:\\n```\\nfor Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it.\\n```\\nwith:\\n```\\nfor Tier 3 (standalone ASP.NET Core hosts), call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()`. These two calls replace the six individual extension methods that existed in earlier beta versions and register request-ID correlation, version headers, and inbound request logging in one step.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new snippet demonstrating `PlatformHeaders` usage in a Tier 3 (standalone) setup.\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample3_PlatformHeaders.cs`\\n\\nCreate this file:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core;\\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder;\\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample3_PlatformHeaders\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Illustrative sample — not a runnable test\\u0026#34;)]\\n    public void PlatformHeaders_Tier3Setup()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders\\n        var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\\n\\n        // AddAgentServerCore registers RequestIdMiddleware, server-version header, and inbound logging.\\n        builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\\n\\n        var app = builder.Build();\\n        app.UseAgentServerCore();\\n\\n        // Read the resolved request-correlation ID from HttpContext.Items in your own middleware.\\n        app.Use(async (context, next) =\\u0026gt;\\n        {\\n            if (context.Items.TryGetValue(PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey, out var requestId))\\n            {\\n                // requestId matches the x-request-id response header for this request.\\n                context.Response.Headers[PlatformHeaders.RequestId] = requestId?.ToString();\\n            }\\n            await next(context);\\n        });\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd a snippet placeholder in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` inside the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Using PlatformHeaders constants in a Tier 3 setup\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. The changes above are additions within existing sections, so no sections need to be created or reordered.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content from the new test file into the README placeholder\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm the API listing is current\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T20:07:23.3152053Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T20:05:16.7050186Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":23,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service
